project.title: African Penguin (Spheniscus demersus)  Plasma Samples
project.projectDescription: Fractionation of normal and abnormal African penguin samples via capillary electrophoresis demonstrated a change from a left skewed composition to a right skewed composition. Further analysis of this fraction via mass spectrometry revealed key proteins associated with this skewed.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: After capillary electrophoresis samples were denatured in 6M urea, reduced in 10 mM DTT, alkylated in 15 mM IAA and quenched with 20 mM DTT. Samples were digested using Trypsin, followed by desalting and protein enrichment.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: All samples were analyzed using Proteome Discoverer 2.0 for protein identification. Statistical analysis was done using MetaboAnalyst 4.0.

references.referenceLine: Valdivia AO, Ortega KJ, Bhattacharya SK, Cray C. Capillary Electrophoresis Assessment of Plasma Protein Changes in an African Penguin (Spheniscus demersus) With Aspergillosis. ACS Omega. 2020 5(51):33280-33289

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[Specimen]: a banked plasma sample approximately one year previous when the animal was in good health.
Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica: for a total of 3 technical replicates.
Comment[FractionationMethod]: Fractions were separated using the Agilent Capillary Electrophoresis 7100 coupled with a bare fused silica capillary tube (Agilent, G1600–63311).
Comment[Separation]: Easy nLC 1000 nano-liquid chromatography system (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[GradientTime]: 20 min of 2% solvent B, 20 min of 30% solvent B, 15 min of 40% solvent B, 20 min of 70% solvent B, and 30 min of 98% solvent B, all at a constant rate flow of 350 nL/min.
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: Desalting and protein enrichment was carried out using the Pierce Graphite Spin Columns (Thermo, 88302)

Characteristics[Specimen]: plasma obtained on the recheck date

Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: Desalting and protein enrichment was carried out using the Pierce Graphite Spin Columns (Thermo, 88302)
